title: Implement a typed Grabowski operation lifecycle vertical slice
goal: Expose operation.start, operation.status, operation.continue, operation.review and operation.close as one thin typed Grabowski lifecycle over existing Bureau tasks, leases, durable executions, review evidence, readbacks and cleanup without introducing a second task or repository truth.

approval.note: The slice must compose existing authorities and receipts; it may not create generic shell, merge, deployment or task-truth authority.

acceptance.assertion: All five calls address one stable operation identity bound to the canonical Bureau task, repository, branch or deployment target, expected base and active resource leases.
Each call returns a bounded typed result with current phase, terminality, effect_started, ambiguity, required_readback, evidence references and explicit non-claims; prose parsing is unnecessary.

The lifecycle reuses existing Bureau task truth, Grabowski durable tasks, resource leases, review gates, GitHub readbacks and checkout lifecycle tools; no operation call can bypass their independent policy checks.
operation.continue resumes only a reconciled non-terminal phase, rejects stale generation or changed bindings and never blindly retries a mutation after an ambiguous result.
operation.review binds exact diff and evidence hashes, while operation.close requires terminal readbacks, releases only owned leases and archives or cleans only eligible owned checkouts.
A fresh operator session can discover and use the vertical slice to progress one real repository task from start through close without human shell assistance or a hidden second status model.

rollback.strategy: Disable the operation adapters and continue using the underlying task, lease, Git, review and cleanup tools directly; retain all durable operation receipts for diagnosis.

metadata.source_finding: The canonical Weltgewebe repository contract now cleanly separates repo contract authority from external operator execution, enabling the first operation lifecycle slice.
